---
title: "Windows Client"
description-meta: "Install and use the openme Windows system-tray app — download, profile setup, and one-click knocking."
keywords: [openme Windows, openme system tray, openme WPF, SPA Windows app, knock Windows]
---

The openme Windows app lives in the system tray. Right-click the icon to knock
without opening a full window.

---

## Download {#download}

[Download for Windows →](../download/windows.qmd)

<!-- Screenshot placeholder: Windows installer or system-tray icon -->
![openme in the Windows system tray](../images/placeholder-windows-tray.png){.placeholder}

---

## Install {#install}

1. Download the `.exe` installer from the [Download page](../download/windows.qmd).
2. Run the installer and follow the wizard.
3. openme starts automatically and appears in the system tray.

---

## Add a Profile {#profile}

1. Right-click the openme system-tray icon.
2. Select **Manage Profiles → Add Profile**.
3. Enter the profile details, or click **Scan QR** and scan the QR code
   generated by `openme add --qr <name>` on the server.

<!-- Screenshot placeholder: Add profile dialog -->
![Add profile dialog](../images/placeholder-windows-add-profile.png){.placeholder}

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Name | Friendly label for this server (e.g. "Home Server") |
| Server | Hostname or IP of the server |
| Port | UDP knock port (default: `54154`) |
| Server public key | Curve25519 public key — from `openme add` output |
| Your private key | Ed25519 private key — generated by `openme add` |

---

## Send a Knock {#knock}

1. Right-click the openme system-tray icon.
2. Click the profile name to knock immediately.

<!-- Screenshot placeholder: Context menu with knock options -->
![System-tray knock menu](../images/placeholder-windows-knock.png){.placeholder}

::: {.callout-note}
The app sends a UDP packet and does not wait for a response — the server never
replies. A toast notification confirms the packet was sent.
:::
